title: Please, Check!
objective: Survive a 3-minute accuracy-review shift by checking the orb packets that break the current requested rule, building combos while avoiding correct packets.

controls.input: Tap a moving orb packet once
controls.outcome: The packet is stamped CHECKED, you score points, and your combo rises.

controls.input: Watch the top banner and pulse color
controls.outcome: You know which mistake type matters right now: color, spin, count, or mirror.

controls.input: Quickly tap the next valid target
controls.outcome: You stop the pressure meter from filling and rebuild your multiplier.

steps: A bold banner announces a request such as PLEASE CHECK: SPIN with a matching icon and voice cue.
Neon orb packets drift in from the edges; each packet shows a simple visual rule pair so the mistake is readable at a glance.
Tap only the packets that fail the active check before they fade out.
Every few seconds a new please-check request interrupts play and changes what counts as a target.
Miss too many faulty packets or tap too many correct ones and your review meter empties, ending the run.
scoring: Each correct check adds points and increases a combo multiplier up to x8. A wrong tap or missed faulty packet resets the combo and removes one review charge. Final score goes to a simple local offline leaderboard for one
tips: Follow the banner first; the target rule changes often enough that old habits get punished.
Faulty packets carry one exaggerated mismatch, so look for the single broken detail instead of reading the whole packet.
Use request-change moments to decide whether to chase a far target or let it go and prepare for the new rule.

trendContext.title: Please check my quantum physics browser game for accuracy
